news.title: Musi oustees to get 2BHK houses: Telangana housing minister
news.para: Telangana's Revenue Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy has assured families displaced by the Musi rejuvenation project will receive 2 BHK homes and lawful compensation. The ambitious project aims to transform the Musi River into a tourism and economic hub for Hyderabad, ensuring flood control, water treatment, and enhanced green spaces.  The government emphasized a humane approach, prioritizing the welfare of the poor and preventing any injustice in the name of development.

news.title: NGT ban triggers sand shortage, prices surge across Jharkhand
news.para: Sand prices have skyrocketed in Jharkhand, with truckloads costing up to Rs 55,000, due to the National Green Tribunal's mining ban until October 15. This has stalled construction projects and forced builders to source sand from Bihar, increasing costs significantly. Developers cite a lack of policy action and alleged black-marketing as contributing factors to the ongoing shortage and price hike, impacting lower economic groups.

news.title: Land conversion norms set for major overhaul to boost business in Rajasthan
news.para: Rajasthan's revenue department is set to simplify land use changes, allowing conversions in planned and unplanned areas with a fee, eliminating separate approvals. This move aims to boost ease of doing business by reducing regulatory hurdles and delays that have long troubled industries. The reforms, part of the Centre's Business Reforms Action Plan, are expected to enhance transparency, speed up project approvals, and attract more private investment to the state.

news.title: Maharashtra deputy CM sets three-year deadline for housing projects
news.para: Maharashtra Deputy Chief Minister Eknath Shinde announced a three-year deadline for developers to complete housing and redevelopment projects. Builders adhering to timelines and quality will receive priority for future government contracts. Shinde emphasized the government's commitment to clearing administrative hurdles and accelerating cluster redevelopment, aiming to make Mumbai and Thane slum-free with quality housing for citizens.

news.title: Gujarat to take back Infocity land, won’t extend lease beyond 2030
news.para: Gujarat government will not renew the lease for the Gandhinagar Infocity project, set to revert to state control in 2030. This decision stems from alleged breaches of lease and concession agreements by developer Creative Infocity Ltd. The government plans to compensate the developer for infrastructure, though likely less than liabilities from violations.

news.title: Fire lays bare risks of living in unfinished societies
news.para: A fire in Noida's Aranya Society highlights the perilous conditions faced by thousands in stalled housing projects. Residents, forced to move into incomplete homes due to financial pressures, are living without essential safety features and civic amenities. This incident has intensified calls for mandatory fire safety audits and stricter accountability for builders and insolvency professionals, emphasizing the urgent need to prioritize resident safety over administrative delays.

news.title: Haryana defers rollout of new occupation certificates certification system
news.para: Haryana's proposed self-certification system for building approvals is on hold. The state will continue with existing rules until a digital infrastructure and administrative framework are established. A dedicated online portal is being developed to process applications and make occupation certificates publicly accessible. High-risk buildings will require third-party certification from experienced architects. Additional FAR benefits will only be granted after charges are notified.

news.title: Odisha RERA retaining entire registration fee is arbitrary: OREAT
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Appellate Tribunal has ruled that the state's real estate authority cannot keep all project registration fees if applications are rejected or withdrawn late. The tribunal ordered refunds for two developers, allowing the authority to retain only 12% as processing charges. This decision aims to prevent unjust enrichment and promote fair practices in real estate development.

news.title: Delhi: Builder ‘abandons’ commercial project after keeping buyers waiting for decades
news.para: A builder has informed Delhi RERA that it has abandoned a commercial project at Central Square Complex after over 30 years, citing a lack of sanctioned building plans. Purearth Infrastructure Ltd, a joint venture, is now offering refunds with interest to buyers who have been waiting for their promised units. This comes after years of delays and ongoing litigation, leaving customers in shock.

news.title: Corporate giants turn to urban regeneration as redevelopment race intensifies
news.para: India's largest corporate houses are focusing more and more on urban regeneration, shifting real estate focus from land acquisition to redevelopment. Giants like Reliance, Adani and JSW are aggressively bidding for projects involving old housing colonies and slum rehabilitation in Mumbai.

news.title: Godrej Properties acquires 47 acre land parcel in Chennai
news.para: Godrej Properties has secured a significant 47-acre land parcel in South Chennai's OMR region, planning a residential development with a projected 1.2 million sq ft of space. This venture is expected to generate ₹500 crore in revenue. The development will primarily feature plotted residential units.

news.title: West Bengal government sends three-page SOP form to builders for construction audit
news.para: Kolkata's state government has issued a new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) requiring builders to submit detailed project information to borough offices, a move welcomed by major developers for its decentralised approach. This initiative aims to prevent future incidents like the Taratala warehouse collapse. Developers are cooperating fully, ensuring key personnel are available for site inspections to expedite project clearances and resume construction work promptly.

news.title: Maharashtra infuses another ₹30 crore, takes metro phase-II equity to ₹607 crore
news.para: Maharashtra government has released an additional ₹30 crore for the Nagpur Metro Phase-2, bringing its total equity contribution to ₹607 crore. This funding supports the 43.8-km project, which recently saw a proposed 1.4-km extension of the Kamptee corridor approved. Land acquisition is progressing well, with over 50% secured, and critical construction milestones are being met, indicating steady momentum for the ambitious metro expansion.

news.title: Building homes around customers: VTP Realty's ₹14,000-crore launch portfolio
news.para: Delivering 8,000+ homes and over 10 million sq. ft. this fiscal, and the company's outlook on future opportunities in the Mumbai market.

news.title: Protest erupts in LDA colony over proposed Lucknow civic body's 60-shop vending zone
news.para: Residents of LDA Colony protested a proposed vending zone in Aashiana, claiming it's a permanent structure violating planning norms.  Locals allege the Lucknow Municipal Corporation is building over 60 shops, despite official claims of a temporary setup.  Concerns are raised about lost footpath space, increased congestion near hospitals and schools, and potential legal action if construction isn't halted.

news.title: Ghaziabad: After fire safety push in condos, RWAs flag high costs, lack of residents’ cooperation, old infra
news.para: Resident welfare associations in Ghaziabad are struggling to meet stringent fire safety directives, citing prohibitive costs for maintaining equipment, outdated electrical systems, and difficulties in keeping emergency exits clear.  Developers often hand over projects before full compliance, leaving associations with hefty upgrade bills and resident reluctance to pay.  Governance gaps and a lack of consistent enforcement further complicate efforts to ensure safety.

news.title: JK Cement to focus on higher capacity utilisation, premiumisation in FY27: CEO
news.para: JK Cement is aggressively pursuing a 50 MnTPA capacity target by 2030, focusing on boosting plant utilization and premium product sales to counter rising costs. The company recently expanded its grey cement capacity to 32.26 MnTPA and is on track with new integrated and grinding units.  A key strategy involves strengthening its value-added products portfolio, including white cement and ready-mix concrete, to enhance market presence and profitability.

news.title: Omaxe plans ₹6,200 crore investment to develop 19 hotels
news.para: Omaxe plans to develop 19 hotels across 13 cities in five states over the next four-five years. The proposed hospitality portfolio will cover nearly five million sq ft and will be developed across urban centres, pilgrimage destinations and transit corridors.

news.title: UK's Bridgepoint to buy Kayne Anderson Real Estate in $1.39 billion deal
news.para: London-based Bridgepoint is acquiring U.S. firm Kayne Anderson's real estate arm for approximately $1.4 billion. This strategic move aims to diversify income and boost international reach, adding $22 billion in property assets to Bridgepoint's existing $117 billion portfolio. The deal, comprising cash and new shares, is expected to significantly enhance Bridgepoint's earnings and solidify its global presence in private markets.

news.title: Ahead of monsoon, MCD surveys 28 lakh buildings in Delhi, flags just 19 as 'dangerous'
news.para: Delhi's recent pre-monsoon survey, which examined around 28 lakh buildings, identified a scant 19 structures deemed hazardous, igniting concerns about its validity. Despite the Municipal Corporation of Delhi asserting an 85% completion rate, experts denounce the purely observational approach. Moreover, the unfortunate reality of building collapses occurring in reportedly safe buildings emphasizes the glaring inadequacies of this yearly review process.
